assertionResults.reason: docker compose ps showed db/rabbit/mailhog/app on approved ports 5433/5672/8025/8081/9090, http://localhost:9090/actuator/health returned 200, and GET /api/v1/auth/me returned 403 as an allowed controlled runtime result.
Management health and readiness returned 200 on port 9090, the app port returned 404 for /actuator/health, and management /actuator/metrics and /actuator/prometheus both returned 403.

With valid config the compose runtime returned readiness 200/UP; after restarting the app with blank ERP_LICENSE_KEY and invalid mail credentials, /actuator/health/readiness returned HTTP 503 with {"status":"DOWN"}; the runtime was then restored to readiness 200/UP.
Strict prod,flyway-v2 compose smoke passed with 9090 health/readiness and 8081 /api/v1/auth/me=403, and the negative required-config check drove readiness DOWN instead of faking green.

commandsRun.command: docker compose ps + curl probes for 9090/health, 9090/readiness, 8081/auth/me, 8081/actuator/health, 9090/metrics, 9090/prometheus
commandsRun.observation: compose ps showed all approved services up; health=200 readiness=200 auth=403 app_actuator=404 metrics=403 prometheus=403.

commandsRun.command: docker compose stop app && docker compose up -d --build app with invalid ERP_LICENSE_KEY and invalid mail credentials
commandsRun.observation: readiness probe returned HTTP 503 and body {"status":"DOWN"}; after restoring valid env the runtime returned health=200 readiness=200 auth=403.

frictions: Task-based flow-validator subagents could not be used in this session because the configured user-testing-flow-validator droid reported an invalid model (gpt-5.4); the parent validator performed equivalent checks directly and captured the same artifacts.
